title: chenopodium strictum subsp. microphyllum
intro:
Chenopodium strictum subsp. microphyllum, also known as dwarf breadroot or small-leaved breadroot, is a perennial plant species belonging to the amaranth family, characterized by its small leaves and edible roots.

The medicinal properties of Chenopodium strictum subsp. microphyllum lie in its roots, which contain saponins and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.

This plant offers several health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a valuable addition to traditional herbal remedies.

Chenopodium strictum subsp. microphyllum can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of uses, from culinary to medicinal applications.
